paper.title: Induction heating of electrically conductive porous asphalt concrete
paper.authors: Quantao Liu; Erik Schlangen; Alvaro Garcia; Martin van de Ven
paper.material_system: Electrically conductive porous asphalt concrete with steel fibers or steel wool
paper.notes: The supplied packet concerns porous asphalt concrete and reports indirect tensile strength, electrical resistivity and induction heating, not cement/concrete compressive strength.

evidence.evidence_text: Induction heating of electrically conductive porous asphalt concrete. The paper examines electrical conductivity and indirect tensile strength of conductive porous asphalt concrete.
evidence.supports: paper identity and non-cement/asphalt material system

evidence.evidence_text: Indirect tensile strength tests at 5 C were conducted on cylindrical porous asphalt samples to characterize the effect of steel wool volume content.
evidence.supports: mechanical property is indirect tensile strength, not compressive strength

evidence.evidence_text: The reported strength results are indirect tensile strength values of porous asphalt concrete with steel fibers or steel wool.
evidence.supports: exclusion of non-compressive, non-cement strength data

main_risks: No cementitious compressive-strength data are present in the supplied packet.
Indirect tensile strength values are intentionally excluded by the extraction prompt.
